observedItems.contentExcerpt: Contents NEWS AND EVENTS > Announcements > Academic Affairs Academic Affairs [Fall 2026] UIC-FIRST Course Registration Procedure Jul 2, 2026 | Views: 1,333 2026-2 UIC First list(UD+QRM)_260702.pdf (564.52KB) [Fall 2026] UIC-FIRST Course Registration Procedure Starting from the 2024 Fall semester, registration for UIC-FIRST courses will no longer be conducted via Google Forms. Instead, it will be managed through the Yonsei Portal System. 1. Enrollment period: From 11:00 AM on Wednesday, August 5

qualitySignals.normalizedText: Contents NEWS AND EVENTS > Announcements > Academic Affairs Academic Affairs [Fall 2026] UIC-FIRST Course Registration Procedure Jul 2, 2026 | Views: 1,333 2026-2 UIC First list(UD+QRM)_260702.pdf (564.52KB) [Fall 2026] UIC-FIRST Course Registration Procedure Starting from the 2024 Fall semester, registration for UIC-FIRST courses will no longer be conducted via Google Forms. Instead, it will be managed through the Yonsei Portal System. 1. Enrollment period: From 11:00 AM on Wednesday, August 5 to 10:59 AM on Thursday, August 6, 2026 (KST). 2. Eligibility: Only students who have declared ECON, PSIR or QRM as their 1st/2nd major, or who are expected to be assigned these majors, are eligible to apply for UIC-FIRST courses during this period. - Students on a leave of absence should be approved for return before 12:00 pm (noon), Tuesday, Aug. 4. Otherwise, they may not be eligible for the UIC- FIRST course enrollment. - Students who received an academic warning should finish their advising session before 12:00 pm (noon), Tuesday, Aug. 4. 3. Procedure a) Log on to Yonsei Portal system (http://portal.yonsei.ac.kr/) - Click on the ‘Undergraduate Course Enrollment’ - Students may allocate up to 12 mileage points per course b) The system will close precisely at the designated time. To ensure fairness for all students, late enrollments will not be accepted under any circumstances. Please note that enrollment delays due to technical issues cannot be accommodated. 4. Enrollment Confirmation a) If enrollment for a course exceeds the seating capacity of the assigned classrooms, we will consult with the course instructor to request an increase in the available slot. If an increase is not approved, enrollment priority will be determined according to the following criteria: i. Students who have applied for graduation or completion (the application page will appear before enrollment). ii. Students with a high ratio of earned credits to the total credits required for graduation. iii. Students with a higher number of credits earned in the previous semester. b) If the application is submitted correctly, UIC-FIRST courses will be enrolled automatically. Students may check their enrolled FIRST courses on the first day of the regular course registration period. c) Upon enrollment, four times the course credits (3 credits * 4 = 12 mileage points per course) will be automatically deducted from the total available mileage. As a result, students who utilize this policy will have reduced mileage during the regular course registration period. 5. Important Notes a) During the enrollment period, students must apply for UIC-FIRST courses only. Any enrollment records for non-UIC First courses will be canceled without prior notice. b) QRM students admitted in 2021 or earlier may also apply for the UIC ECON First courses offered in UD as Non-QRM. This is not applicable to students admitted in 2022 and thereafter. c) If you are retaking a course, the retake option(재수강횟수) will not be applied automatically. Please ensure that you check whether the course is retake and manually select the retake option on the portal during the regular course registration period. d) Course information, including the instructor, time, and location, is subject to change. Please refer to the course catalog on the Yonsei portal for the most up-to-date information. 6. Contact information: 02-2123-3925 (UD) / 032-749-3760 (HASS) List Prev [ALL] Leave & Return Application Guidelines for Fall 2026 2026-07-02 Next [ALL] UIC Scholarship Application Announcement: 2026 Fall 2026-06-16 NEWS AND EVENTS Announcements Overview Academic Affairs Student Services Event Careers UIC Newsletter

qualitySignals.normalizedText: UIC Scholarship Application Announcement: 2026 Fall * From 2022 winter, documents for Need-based Scholarship(언더우드자유장학금) should be submitted via Yonsei Portal System(http://portal.yonsei.ac.kr) during the application period. (Log in -> Academic Information -> Scholarship -> Students Scholarship Application(학생장학신청) -> Select 2026-2 semester ->Step 1. Applicable Scholarship -> 언더우드자유장학금 -> Download Notice File -> ADD -> Step 2. Apply for Scholarship ->Upload Application Form (Notice File) and Supplementary Documents in combined PDF file-> Apply) * Note: Merit-based scholarship(진리장학금) applicants do NOT need to submit a scholarship application except for students currently on leave and are planning to return in the 2026 Fall Semester. 1. Types of Scholarship and Eligibility A. Common Requirements - Minimum of 12 credits in 2026 Spring Semester (or the most current semester for those on leave of absence) - No overlapping scholarships are permitted except for 국가장학금 (the scholarship provided by Korea Student Aid Foundation) - Students who have completed more than 8 semesters as of 2026 Spring Semester cannot apply. B. Merit-based Scholarship (성적우수장학금) - Outstanding 2026 Spring Semester (or the most current semester for those on leave of absence) GPA C. Need-based Scholarship (가계곤란장학금) - 2026 Spring Semester (or the most current semester for those on leave of absence) GPA 2.5 or higher D. ESP (Exchange Student Program) Scholarship (교환학생장학금) - 2026 Spring Semester (or the most current semester for those on leave of absence) GPA 2.5 or higher - Outbound exchange students who have been selected to be dispatched in the 2026 Fall Semester 2. Amount: Full tuition, 1/2 of the tuition, 1/3 of the tuition, etc. 3. Selection Criteria A. Merit-based: Academic excellence (GPA) B. Need-based: Financial need C. ESP: Students who meet the above qualifications will be automatically selected without any additional application procedures. * Students who plan to return from leave of absence (복학생) should submit a merit-based scholarship application (attached) via email to each division’s office(UD: uic_ud@yonsei.ac.kr / HASS: uic_hass@yonsei.ac.kr ISE: uic_ise@yonsei.ac.k ) by July 16th, 2026. It is each student's responsibility to check the application status with each division’s office.(UD: 02-2123-3925 / HASS: 032-749-3760 / ISE: 032-749-3801) 4. Need-based Scholarship Application Period A. 9AM June 18th (Thu) ~ 5PM July 16th (Thu), 2026 (Korea Standard Time) B. Documents for need-based scholarship must be submitted via Yonsei Portal during the application period. 5. Application Materials for Need-Based Scholarship [Korean Students] 1) 가계곤란장학금신청서 (Scholarship Application Form: Attached) : 학사포탈에서 다운로드하여 반드시 영문으로 작성 2) 기타 가계곤란 증빙서류{파산증명서, 부채증명서, 직계가족의 장기입원확인증명서, 실직증명서(구직등록필증-고용안정센터 발급) 등} 각 1부(해당자만) 3) 근로소득 원천징수 영수증, 건강보험료 납부확인서, 지방세세목별과세증명서 등은 제출할 필요 없음. 4) 제출 증빙서류가 여러개인 경우, 지원서양식을 제외한 모든 증빙서류를 병합하여 하나의 PDF파일로 제출 (반드시 A4사이즈로 통일) 5) 국가장학금 (I, II유형)을 반드시 신청해야 함. 국가장학금 미신청자는 가계곤란장학금 심사 대상에서 제외됨. (횟수초과 등으로 국가장학금 신청불가자는 국가장학금 신청 없이 신청가능. 단, 지원서에 해당 사항 명시) * 한국장학재단 산정 소득분위가 있을 경우 소득분위 확인서로 증빙서류를 갈음함. [Non-Korean students] The following should be prepared in English. 1) Scholarship Application Form: Downloadable from the Yonsei Portal 2) Tax return statements from both you and your parent(s) or guardian(s) (if applicable) 3) A copy of a salary statement from both you and/or your guardian(s) for the last six or twelve months (if applicable) 4) Other income/asset information documents (if applicable) 5) Any other documents proving a need for financial aid 6) All the documents mentioned above shoud be merged into 1 PDF file except for the application form. * It is the student's responsibility to convince the school of true financial need. The more supporting documents you can submit the higher your chances of success will be. If possible, for example, you should submit copies of your banking and credit card statements for the past 6 months (12 months preferred), your housing lease agreement, statements of unemployment and bankruptcy by your guardian, and income tax and estate tax statements by yourself and/or guardian. All documents not in English or Korean must be translated into English or Korean. How to Submit the Documents on Yonsei Portal Click the Download button on the Scholarship Application Form -> Fill out and Save Downloaded Application -> Upload Saved Application > Click the 'Apply' button at the bottom 1) Please scan the supplementary documents or take clear photos with your cell phone, upload them online with the scholarship application form. 2) If there are multiple documents for submission, merge them into one PDF file and make sure to standardize the size to A4. 3) Upload file capacity is limited to 10MB. If the file has a larger capacity, please send them to your division office via e-mail under the title of "2026-2 Needbased_Student ID_Name.pdf". 6. Interview schedules for the need-based scholarship will be announced individually, if necessary. 7. Results Announcement: 2026 Fall Tuition Fee Bill Print Period through Bill Print Menu on Yonsei Portal System 8. Scholarship payment method: Automatic deduction on tuition statements For any questions or concerns, please contact to each division’s office. (UD: uic_ud@yonsei.ac.kr / HASS: uic_hass@yonsei.ac.kr / ISE: uic_ise@yonsei.ac.kr ) -Underwood International College-

qualitySignals.normalizedText: Contents NEWS AND EVENTS > Announcements > Academic Affairs Academic Affairs [ALL] Exchange Student Program(ESP) for Spring 2027 (2027학년도 봄학기 해외파견프로그램) Jun 10, 2026 | Views: 1,796 List of Host Universities for the 2027 Spring (Click) Please refer to the OIA's announcement for the full list of host universities (including the UIC-exclusive partners) for Spring 2027: https://oia.yonsei.ac.kr/outbound.php?mid=m04_01&cmid=m05_02&skeyword=&pact=&sHeader=&act=view&uid=10892&page=1 OIA Outbound Program Info Session for Spring 2027 The UIC-Exclusive Exchange Program is administered in conjunction with the Office of International Affairs’ exchange student application process. Accordingly, during the official exchange application period, UIC-Exclusive Exchange partner universities appear in the portal system together with other exchange partner universities managed by the Office of International Affairs. Please note that the information session video and materials on this page are in Korean. Please download the bilingual guidebook (Korean/English) linked below for details on eligibility, procedures and deadlines. * Application period for Spring 2027 exchange programs: 10 AM, July 9 (Thu) ~ 5 PM, 10 (Fri), 2026 (KST) CLICK >> 2027-1 OIA Info Session Booklet(KOR/ENG).pdf Since most of the application and dispatch procedures are the same as those for the regular exchange programs administered by the Office of International Affairs, students should refer to the Office’s general exchange program guidelines. However, inquiries regarding UIC-Exclusive Exchange partner universities should be directed to the UIC Office, while inquiries regarding other exchange partner universities should be directed to the Office of International Affairs. * Contact: UIC Exclusive exchange: uic_services@yonsei.ac.kr/02-2123-3922 U-wide exchange: exch@yonsei.ac.kr / 02-2123-8453 *Please note that schedules are subject to change depending on circumstances at the host country or university. For information on the tuition reduction scholarship benefit available to UIC students participating in exchange programs, please refer to the linked page. The benefit is applied automatically to eligible students, and no separate application is required. (ACADEMICS > Exclusive Programs > Study Abroad > Overview>Exchange>Scholarship) https://uic.yonsei.ac.kr/main/academic.php?mid=m03_05_01 2027학년도 봄학기 해외파견프로그램 배정대학 명단 (Click) 국제처 공지사항에 게시된 2027 봄학기 배정가능 대학 명단(UIC 단독교환교 포함)을 확인해주시기 바랍니다: https://oia.yonsei.ac.kr/outbound.php?mid=m04_01&cmid=m05_02&skeyword=&pact=&sHeader=&act=view&uid=10892&page=1 [2027학년도 봄학기 해외파견프로그램 설명회] UIC-Exclusive Exchange 프로그램은 국제처의 교환학생 파견 프로그램 신청과 통합 운영되고 있습니다. 따라서 국제처의 교환학생 파견 신청 기간 중 포탈 시스템을 통해 국제처 주관 파견대학들과 함께 신청 가능 목록에 표시됩니다. 2026. 5. 7.(목) 국제처에서 실시한「2026학년도 봄학기 해외파견프로그램 설명회」의 영상과 발표 자료를 아래 링크에서 확인하실 수 있습니다. - 해외파견 프로그램 소개, 지원 일정·자격·절차, 장학금, 파견대학 명단 안내 (39분) - TOEFL iBT 시험 안내 및 주요 개정 사항 특별 강연 – ETS Korea (13분) - 챕터별 영상 바로가기 및 안내책자 다운로드 제공 <<설명회 영상 및 자료 바로가기>> * 2027-1학기 해외파견프로그램 접수기간: 2026년 7월 9일(목) 10:00 ~ 10일(금) 17:00 * 문의: 대부분의 파견 절차는 국제처 교환학생 파견 절차와 동일하므로, 기본적인 신청 및 파견 절차는 위 국제처 안내문을 참고해 주시면 됩니다. 다만, UIC-Exclusive Exchange 파견교에 대한 세부 문의는 UIC 행정팀으로, 그 외 국제처 주관 파견대학 관련 문의는 국제처로 해주시기 바랍니다. UIC 단독교환: UIC 행정팀 uic_services@yonsei.ac.kr / 02-2123-3922 U-wide 교환: 국제처 국제팀 02-2123-8453 / exch@yonsei.ac.kr *본 해외파견 프로그램은 파견국가의 상황 또는 대학 내부 상황으로 인해 일정 등에 변동이 있을 수 있으므로 이점 양지하여 주시기 바랍니다. UIC 학생의 교환 파견 시 등록금 감액 장학 혜택에 관한 내용은 본 링크를 참고해 주시기 바랍니다. 해당 장학 혜택은 조건을 충족하는 학생에게 자동 적용되며, 별도의 신청 절차는 없습니다: (ACADEMICS > Exclusive Programs > Study Abroad > Overview>Exchange>Scholarship) https://uic.yonsei.ac.kr/main/academic.php?mid=m03_05_01 List Prev [ALL] UIC Scholarship Application Announcement: 2026 Fall 2026-06-16 Next Important Notice Regarding Major Elective Courses for LSBT Students 2025-06-25 NEWS AND EVENTS Announcements Overview Academic Affairs Student Services Event Careers UIC Newsletter

observedItems.contentExcerpt: [Declaring / Changing the 1st Major and Double Major] UIC Humanities, Arts, and Social Sciences (HASS) Division Students in their 2nd semester are required to declare a major according to the following guidelines. HASS Division students who have declared their first major may change it within HASS majors by the end of their 7th semester, but may only do this once. ■ Application Period: June 23(Tue) 9:00 ~ June 29(Mon), 17:00, 2026 Major declaration will be reflected on Yonsei Portal as of the fi

qualitySignals.normalizedText: [Declaring / Changing the 1st Major and Double Major] UIC Humanities, Arts, and Social Sciences (HASS) Division Students in their 2nd semester are required to declare a major according to the following guidelines. HASS Division students who have declared their first major may change it within HASS majors by the end of their 7th semester, but may only do this once. ■ Application Period: June 23(Tue) 9:00 ~ June 29(Mon), 17:00, 2026 Major declaration will be reflected on Yonsei Portal as of the first day of the next semester. Even though students’ major does not show up on the Yonsei Portal before the above date, during the registration period, students’ mileage will be vetted according to the major they applied for. ■ UIC HASS Division Major Declaration 1. First Major UIC HASS Division students can choose their 1st major among the following 8 majors. ① Asian Studies (AS) ② Culture & Design Management (CDM) ③ Information & Interaction Design (IID) ④ Creative Technology Management (CTM) ⑤ Justice and Civil Leadership (JCL) ⑥ Quantitative Risk Management (QRM) ⑦ Science, Technology, and Policy (STP) ⑧ Sustainable Development and Cooperation (SDC) * Students will be accepted to the major of their choice without any selection process. 2. Second Major (Optional) UIC HASS Division students may apply for their 2nd major among the UIC HASS 8 majors as well as among any majors offered at Yonsei which has announced open spots on the portal. HASS students may also apply for their 2nd major among UIC Underwood Division (UD) and Integrated Science and Engineering Division (ISED). If they apply for their 2nd major within UIC HASS, they will be accepted to the 2nd major of their choice without any selection process. However, there is a selection process for UD and ISED majors, as well as majors offered outside of UIC, and students must submit a "Reason for Application & Desired Career" and a "Study Plan" through the Portal. ■ Applicable Students 1. First Major • Declaring 1st Major: Students in their 2nd semester • Changing 1st Major within HASS Division: Students must declare their 1st major at the end of their 2nd semester and may change it within HASS by the end of their 7th semester *HASS students cannot change their first major to UD or ISED. 2. Second Major (Optional) Declaring 2nd Major: Students (including those on a leave of absence) can apply for a double major after they complete the third semester up until the semester prior to the final semester (the previous semester of the graduating semester, 졸업직전학기). Changing 2nd Major: Students who have already declared their 2nd major (including those on a leave of absence) can apply for a change of double major up until the semester prior to the final semester (the previous semester of the graduating semester, 졸업직전학기). * In order to change 2nd major, students need to withdraw their current double major on the portal system ‘Application for cancellation of double major’ before the double major application period begins. ■ How to Declare Your Major 1. Go to the online Yonsei Portal Services at http://portal.yonsei.ac.kr/main/index.jsp 2. Click on "Academic Information System” and log in. 3. Choose the “Academic Affairs” menu from the top menu and then click on “UIC Major Declaration" from the Student column. 4. Select your 1st major and click the “Application” button ■ How to Apply for a 2nd Major within UIC (Optional) 1. Go to the online Yonsei Portal Services at http://portal.yonsei.ac.kr/main/index.jsp 2. Click on "Academic Information System” and log in. 3. Choose the “Academic Affairs” menu from the top menu. In order to declare/apply for a 2nd major within UIC, click on “UIC Double Major Declaration” A. To declare a 2nd major within HASS: 1) Select your major and click the "Application" button. 2) You will be accepted to the applied 2nd major within HASS Division and you can confirm by checking the Application statement. B. To apply for a 2nd major from UD and ISED: 1) Select your desired major. 2) Fill out and save the "Reason for Application & Desired Career" and "Study Plan." 3) You can check your application statement on the page. Results will be announced in the beginning of next semester. ■ Guidelines 1. UIC HASS students must declare their 1st major at the end of their 2nd semester and may change it within HASS by the end of their 7th semester. Changing a major is allowed only once. 2. To declare a 2nd major within UIC HASS, students can do so from their 3rd semester and may change it until the semester prior to the final semester(the previous semester of the graduating semester, 졸업직전학기). Students who wish to declare a 2nd major must have their 1st major officially confirmed before applying. 3. To declare a 2nd major from UIC UD and ISED, students are allowed to apply from their 3rd semester and may change it by the semester prior to the final semester(the previous semester of the graduating semester, 졸업직전학기). Students have to undergo a selection process to choose UD and ISED majors as a second major and each major department will select successful candidates among applicants. 4. Declaring majors/changing applications after the designated period is not permitted. 5. Students cannot declare or change his/her first major during a leave of absence. * If you want to re-apply after canceling the current double major, you must cancel your approved double major through the portal system before the double major application period begins. (Double majors can be canceled until the semester before graduation). You cannot apply again for a canceled major. * If you have been approved for a double major (second major), you can apply for a third major during the application period for graduation (or completion) without prior approval. However, if the student fails to fulfill the graduation requirement for a double major (second major), the third major is automatically canceled and graduation (or completion) is not possible. Underwood International College For AS, CDM, IID, CTM students, Tel) 032-749-3761 E-mail) uic_intl.cam@yonsei.ac.kr For JCL, QRM, STP, SDC students, Tel) 032-749-3760 E-mail) uic_hass@yonsei.ac.kr

qualitySignals.normalizedText: [ASD Only] Declaring / Changing Major and Double major for ASD students 1. First Major UIC Asian Studies Division (ASD) students in their 2nd semester do not need to declare the major and will be automatically designated as Asian Studies (AS) majors at the end of their 2nd semester. It will be reflected on Yonsei Portal as of the first day of the next semester. Even though students’ major does not show up on the Yonsei Portal before the above date, during the registration period, students’ mileage will be vetted according to the major they applied for. 2. Second Major (Optional) ■ Application Period: June 23(Tue) 9:00 ~ June 29(Mon), 17:00, 2026 UIC Asian Studies Division (ASD) students may apply for their 2nd major among any majors offered at Yonsei which has announced open spots on the portal. ASD students may also apply for their 2nd major among UIC Underwood Division (UD), 7 other majors within HASS Division, and Integrated Science and Engineering Division (ISED) majors. However, there is a selection process for UD, 7 other majors within the HASS Division, and ISED majors, as well as majors offered outside of UIC. Students must submit a "Reason for Application & Desired Career" and a "Study Plan" through the portal. ■ Applicable Students 1. First Major • Declaring 1st Major: Students in their 2nd semester ASD students will be automatically declared as AS majors at the end of their 2nd semester, and it will be reflected on the Yonsei Portal as of the first day of the next semester. => ASD students cannot change their first major within UIC. 2. Second Major (Optional) • To select UD, 7 other majors within HASS Division, and ISED as a 2nd Major - Declaring 2nd Major: Students (including those on a leave of absence) can apply for a double major from their third semester up until the semester prior to the final semester (the previous semester of the graduating semester, 졸업직전학기). - Changing 2nd Major: Students who have already declared their 2nd major (including those on a leave of absence) can apply for a change of double major up until the semester prior to the final semester (the previous semester of the graduating semester, 졸업직전학기). ■ How to Apply for a 2nd Major within UIC (Optional) 1. Go to the online Yonsei Portal Services at http://portal.yonsei.ac.kr/main/index.jsp 2. Click on "Academic Information System” and log in. 3. Choose the “Academic Affairs” menu from the top menu. In order to declare/apply for a 2nd major within UIC, click on “UIC Double Major Declaration” A. To apply for a 2nd major from UD, 7 other majors of HASS, and ISED: 1) Select your desired major. 2) Fill out and save the "Reason for Application & Desired Career" and "Study Plan." 3) You can check your application statement on the page. Results will be announced in the beginning of next semester. ■ Guidelines 1. UIC ASD students will be automatically declared as AS majors at the end of their 2nd semester, and it will be reflected on Yonsei Portal on the first day of the next semester. UIC ASD students cannot change their 1st major within UIC. 2. To declare a 2nd major from UIC UD, HASS, or ISED, students admitted in 2012 and thereafter are allowed to apply from their 3rd semester and may change it by the semester prior to the final semester (the previous semester of the graduating semester, 졸업직전학기). Students have to undergo a selection process to choose UD, HASS, and ISED majors as a second major and each major department will select successful candidates among applicants. 3. Declaring 2nd majors/changing applications after the designated period will not be accepted. * If you want to re-apply after canceling the current double major, you must cancel your approved double major through the portal system before the double major application period begins. (Double majors can be canceled until the semester before graduation). You cannot apply again for a canceled major. * If you have been approved for a double major (second major), you can apply for a third major during the application period for graduation (or completion) without prior approval. However, if the student fails to fulfill the graduation requirement for a double major (second major), the third major is automatically canceled and graduation (or completion) is not possible. Underwood International College Tel) 032-749-3761 E-mail) uic_intl.cam@yonsei.ac.kr

qualitySignals.normalizedText: [Declaring / Changing Major, Double Major for Integrated Science and Engineering Division Students] UIC Integrated Science and Engineering Division (ISED) Students in their 2nd semester are required to declare a major according to the following guidelines. ISED students who have declared their first major may change it within ISED majors by the end of their 7th semester, but may only do this once. ■ Application Period: June 23(Tue) 9:00 ~ June 29(Mon), 17:00, 2026 Major Declaration will be reflected on Yonsei Portal as of September 1st, 2026. Even though students’ major does not show up on the Yonsei Portal before March 1st, 2026 during the registration period, students’ mileage will be vetted according to the major they applied for. ■ UIC Integrated Science and Engineering Division Major Declaration 1. First Major UIC ISED students can choose their 1st major among the following 3 majors. ① Nano Science and Engineering (NSE) ② Energy & Environmental Science and Engineering (EESE) ③ Bio-Convergence (BC) * Students will be accepted to the major of their choice without any selection process. 2. Second Major (Optional) UIC ISED students who have been assigned their 1st major may apply for a 2nd major among the UIC ISED 3 majors as well as from any major at Yonsei that has announced available spots on the portal. Additionally, ISED students may also apply for their 2nd major in the UIC Underwood Division and HASS Division. If students apply for their 2nd major within UIC ISED, they will be accepted to the 2nd major of their choice without any selection process. However, there is a selection process for UD and HASS majors, as well as majors offered outside of UIC, and students must submit a "Reason for Application & Desired Career" and a "Study Plan" through the Portal. ■ Applicable Students 1. First Major • Declaring 1st Major: Students in their 2nd semester • Changing 1st Major within ISED Division: Students must declare their 1st major at the end of their 2nd semester and may change it within ISED by the end of their 7th semester *ISED students cannot change their first major to UD or HASS. 2. Second Major (Optional) • Declaring 2nd Major: Students who have been assigned their 1st major (including those on a leave of absence) may apply for a double major from their third semester up until the semester prior to their final semester(the semester before their graduating semester, 졸업직전학기). • Changing 2nd Major: Students who have already declared their 2nd major (including those on a leave of absence) can apply for a change of double major up until the semester prior to the final semester(the semester before their graduating semester, 졸업직전학기). *In order to change 2nd major, students need to withdraw their current double major on the portal system ‘Application for cancellation of double major’ before the double major application period begins. ■ How to Declare Your Major 1. Go to the online Yonsei Portal Services at http://portal.yonsei.ac.kr/main/index.jsp 2. Click on "Academic Information System” and log in. 3. Choose the “Academic Affairs” menu from the top menu and then click on “UIC Major Declaration" from the Student column. 4. Select your 1st major and click the “Application” button ■ How to Apply for 2nd Major within UIC (Optional) 1. Go to the online Yonsei Portal Services at http://portal.yonsei.ac.kr/main/index.jsp 2. Click on "Academic Information System” and log in. 3. Choose the “Academic Affairs” menu from the top menu. In order to declare / apply for a 2nd major within UIC, click on “UIC Double Major Declaration” A. To declare 2nd major within ISED: 1) Select your major and click the "Application" button. 2) You will be accepted to the applied 2nd major within ISED Division and you can confirm by checking on the Application statement. B. To apply for 2nd major from UD and HASS: 1) Select your desired major. 2) Fill out and save the "Reason for Application &Desired Career" and "Study Plan." 3) You can check your application statement on the page. Results will be announced in early March. ■ Guidelines 1. UIC ISED students must declare their 1st major at the end of their 2nd semester and may change it within ISED by the end of their 7th semester. Changing the 1st major is allowed only once. 2. Students may declare a 2nd major after being assigned their 1st major and from the 3 semesters onward. They may also change their 2nd major up until the semester prior to their final semester (the semester before their graduating semester, 졸업직전학기). 3. To declare 2nd major from UIC UD and HASS, students are allowed to apply from their 3rd semester and may change it by the semester prior to the final semester(the previous semester of the graduating semester, 졸업직전학기). Students have to undergo a selection process to choose UD and HASS majors as a second major and each major department will select successful candidates among applicants. 4. Declaring majors / changing applications after the designated period is not permitted. 5. Students cannot declare or change their 1st major during leave of absence. * If you want to re-apply after cancelling the approved double major, you must cancel your double major through the portal system before the double major application period begins. (Double majors can be canceled until the semester before graduation). You cannot re-apply again for the cancelled major. * If you have been approved for a double major (second major), you can apply for a third major during the application period for graduation (or completion) without prior approval. However, if the student fails to fulfill the graduation requirement for a double major (second major), the third major is automatically cancelled and graduation (or completion) is not possible. Integrated Science and Engineering Division, Underwood International College Tel) 032-749-3801 E-mail) uic_ise@yonsei.ac.kr

First Major UIC Underwood Division Life Science and Biotechnology (UD LSBT) International students in their 2nd semester are required to declare LSBT major according to the following guidelines. UD LSBT Korean students in their 2nd semester do not need to declare a major and will be automatically designated as Life Science and Biotechnology at the end of their 2nd semester. It will be reflected on Yonsei Portal as of September 1, 2026. 2. Second Major (Optional) UIC UD LSBT students may apply for a 2nd major within Underwood Division, as well as among any majors offered at Yonsei which have announced open spots on the Portal. UD students admitted in 2012 and 2013 may also apply for a 2nd major among UIC Asian Studies Division (ASD), Techno-Art Division (TAD) and UD students admitted in 2014 and thereafter may apply for a 2nd major among UIC Asian Studies Division (ASD), Techno-Art Division (TAD), Integrated Social Sciences Division (ISSD) and Integrated Science and Engineering Division (ISED) majors. If they apply for a 2nd major within UIC UD, they will be accepted to the 2nd major of their choice without any selection process. However, there is a selection process for HASS, ISED majors, as well as majors offered outside of UIC. Students must submit a "Reason for Application & Desired Career" and a "Study Plan" through the Portal. ■ Applicable Students 1. First Major(LSBT International Students Only) • Declaring 1st Major: Students in their 2nd semester LSBT students will be automatically declared as LSBT majors at the end of their 2nd semester, and it will be reflected on the Yonsei Portal as of September 1, 2026. LSBT students cannot change their first major within UIC. 2. Second Major (Optional) • Within UIC UD Majors Declaring 2nd Major: Students(including those on a leave of absence) can apply for a double major from their third semester until the semester prior to the final semester(the previous semester of the graduating semester, 졸업직전학기). Students who wish to declare a 2nd major must have their 1st major officially confirmed before applying. Changing 2nd Major: Students who have already declared their 2nd major (including those on a leave of absence) can apply for a change of double major until the semester prior to the final semester(the previous semester of the graduating semester, 졸업직전학기). *In order to change a 2nd major, you need to withdraw your double major on the portal system ‘Application for cancellation of double major’ before the double major application period begins. ■ How to Declare Your Major 1. Go to the online Yonsei Portal Services at http://portal.yonsei.ac.kr/main/index.jsp 2. Click on "Academic Information System” and log in. 3. Choose the “Academic Affairs” menu from the top menu and then click on “UIC Major Declaration" from the Student column. 4. Select your 1st major and click the “Application” button ■ How to Apply for a 2nd Major within UIC (Optional) 1. Go to the online Yonsei Portal Services at http://portal.yonsei.ac.kr/main/index.jsp 2. Click on "Academic Information System” and log in. 3. Choose the “Academic Affairs” menu from the top menu. In order to declare / apply for a 2nd major within UIC, click on “UIC Double Major Declaration”. A. To declare a 2nd major within UD: 1) Select your major and click the "Application" button. 2) You will be accepted to the applied 2nd major within Underwood Division and you can confirm by checking on the Application statement. B. To apply for a 2nd major from HASS, ISED (applicable to students admitted in 2012 and after): 1) Select your desired major. 2) Fill out and save the "Reason for Application &Desired Career" and "Study Plan." 3) You can check your application statement on the page. Results will be announced in early September. ■ Guidelines 1. UIC students must declare their 1st major at the end of their 2nd semester and may change it within their admissions track by the end of their 7th semester. 2. To declare a 2nd major within UIC UD, UD students can do so from their third semester and may change it until the semester prior to the final semester(the previous semester of the graduating semester, 졸업직전학기). Students who wish to declare a 2nd major must have their 1st major officially confirmed before applying. 3. To declare a 2nd major from UIC HASS, ISED, students admitted in 2012 and thereafter are allowed to apply from their third semester and may change it by the semester prior to the final semester(the previous semester of the graduating semester, 졸업직전학기). Students have to undergo a selection process to choose HASS, ISED majors as a second major and each major department will select successful candidates among applicants. Students who wish to declare a 2nd major must have their 1st major officially confirmed before applying. 4. Declaring majors / changing applications after the designated period is not permitted. 5. Students cannot declare or change his/her major during leave of absence. * If you want to re-apply after cancelling the approved double major, you must cancel your double major through the portal system before the double major application period begins. (Double majors can be cancelled until the semester before graduation). You cannot apply again for a cancelled major. * If you have been approved for a double major (second major), you can apply for a third major during the application period for graduation (or completion) without prior approval. However, if the student fails to fulfill the graduation requirement for a double major (second major), the third major is automatically cancelled and graduation (or completion) is not possible. newNotices.title: [★] 경영학부생 <외부장학 추천> 신청 방법 안내
newNotices.content: 제 목 [★] 경영학부생 <외부장학 추천> 신청 방법 안내 2026.03.05 | 757 첨부파일 등록된 첨부파일이 없습니다. 경영학부 학생 중 외부 장학재단 등 타 기관 장학금 수혜를 위해 경영학부의 추천서가 필요한 학생은 다음과 같이 신청해주시기 바랍니다. 1. 신청시기: 추천서 수령 희망일 기준 최소 7일 전까지 (기한 엄수/ 이후 신청할 경우 희망 수령일 이내로 추천서 승인이 이루어지지 않을 수 있음) 2. 신청절차 1) 기한 내 구글 폼 양식을 통해 신청 후 경영학부 사무실로 전화하여 접수 요청 (https://forms.gle/tYtfGqEw5fQGYSNt9) 2) 수령 희망일 방문 전 경영학부 사무실로 확인 전화 후 방문 수령 문의: 경영학부 사무실 02-820-5539, 5540 목록

newNotices.title: 외국인 유학생 등록금 납부 관련 유의사항 안내
newNotices.content: 홈으로 알림마당 공지사항 공지사항 공지사항 학부소식 채용공고 자료실 공지사항 외국인 유학생 등록금 납부 관련 유의사항 안내 작성일 2026-07-02 안녕하세요, 광고홍보학부 사무실입니다.최근 일부 외국인 유학생이 불법 사설 환전을 통해 등록금을 납부하는 과정에서 학교 계좌가 보이스피싱 등 금융범죄에 연루되어 지급정지되는 사례가 발생하고 있습니다.유학생 여러분께서는 피해를 예방하기 위해 아래 유의사항을 안내드리오니 반드시 확인하시기 바랍니다.- 유의사항 -불법 사설 환전소 및 SNS(위챗 등)를 통한 환전 서비스를 이용하지 마세요.지인을 통한 환전 또는 송금을 하지 마세요.등록금 고지서(가상계좌 정보 포함)를 타인에게 공유하거나 노출하지 마세요.본인 명의의 계좌를 타인에게 빌려주지 마세요.타인 명의의 계좌를 이용하여 등록금을 납부하지 마세요.등록금은 반드시 정식 금융기관을 이용하여 본인 명의의 계좌로 납부하시기 바랍니다.불법 환전이나 타인 명의 계좌를 이용할 경우 금융범죄에 연루될 수 있으며, 본인뿐만 아니라 학교에도 피해가 발생할 수 있습니다.안전한 등록금 납부를 위해 반드시 위 사항을 준수하여 주시기 바랍니다.감사합니다. 이전글 2026학년도 1학기 기말시험 간 부정행위자 처분 공고 다음글 2026학년도 2학기 수강신청(학부) 일정 안내 및 공지 목록으로

newNotices.title: [학생] 외국인 유학생 등록금 납부 관련 유의사항 안내
newNotices.content: [학생] 외국인 유학생 등록금 납부 관련 유의사항 안내 응용통계학과 2026.07.08 조회수 50 최근 외국인 유학생이 불법 사설 환전을 통해 등록금을 납부하는 과정에서 학교 계좌가 보이스피싱 등 금융범죄에 연루되어 지급정지되는 사례가 발생하고 있습니다.유학생 여러분께서는 피해를 예방하기 위해 아래 유의사항을 안내드리오니 확인 바랍니다.- 유의사항 -- 정식 금융기관을 이용하지 않고 불법 사설 환전소 이용하지 말 것.- 지인을 통한 환전 혹은 이체 및 송금하지 말 것.- 등록금 고지서(가상계좌가 표기된)를 타인에게 노출하지 말 것.\- 본인 계좌를 타인에게 빌려주지 말 것.- 타인 계좌로 등록금을 납부하지 말 것.등록금은 반드시 정식 금융기관을 이용하여 본인 명의의 계좌로 납부하시기 바랍니다. 목록
